description_long: This object is a vintage film projector with a robust, boxy metal body and two large film reels mounted on either side. The front features a prominent lens assembly, flanked by a yellow and black striped grill that likely serves as a ventilation or speaker area. The sides display various mechanical components, including a film feed mechanism and a set of control dials and switches. The base is sturdy, with visible wiring and a power supply unit attached, suggesting it is designed for stationary use. The surface is predominantly dark green or black, with metallic accents and some areas of exposed hardware. The overall design is utilitarian, with clear functional elements for film projection and manual operation.
description: A vintage, boxy film projector with large side reels, a prominent lens, and a striped grill, featuring visible control dials and a sturdy base with wiring.
description_view_0: Narrow end view showing the lens assembly and the striped grill, with the film reel and wiring visible on the side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting both large film reels, the full length of the striped grill, and the control panel beneath the main body.
description_view_2: Opposite narrow end view, focusing on the rear of the projector and the film feed mechanism, with less detail on the lens.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the reels, ventilation grills, and the power supply unit, with more visible wiring and hardware.
attribute_lens_type: fixed; medium-sized round lens with a metallic housing, no visible adjustment rings
attribute_control_panel_design: Located below the main body, the control panel features several physical buttons and dials, with some labeled switches and indicator lights, arranged in a linear fashion.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: A prominent yellow and black striped grill on the front, with additional slotted vents on the side panels for airflow.
